approach: Op1.M1 of the cyberfleet-batch self-hosting kernel — spec + build the mission-graph store + ready/cycles engine
new top-level SDD node sdd/mission-graph, single-writer by construction (no contention)
HITL at the spec + impl gates per the operations graph; intra-gate work AFK
spec-then-build: freeze the .feature, then deliver the zero-dep .mts engine + colocated tests over authored fixtures
delegate the impl build to Sonnet
re-emitted here under the SDD node ledger (was removed from the cyberfleet ledger)

why: Cold sdd-spec-judge ALIGNED (oracle/builder/architect PASS) over the new sdd/mission-graph node: git-tracked store (Operations/Missions; RAW/parent-child/discovered-from edges; status; declared node-level touch-sets; schema v:1; tombstone) + zero-dep ready/cycles fold (RAW frontier + node-level WAW-mutex; write-guard + fold-time quarantine) + Operation deliverability check + manual authoring; 36 boolean scenarios over per-scenario constructed fixtures (never the live store) incl. the #135/#136/#137 fixture. New top-level single-node capability (mirrors formation/), concept orchestration. v1 scope tightened at spec time: dropped ready critical-path ranking + soft-overlap output (dispatcher/finer-ladder concerns, deferred). discovered-from kept as a recorded-but-not-folded provenance edge (user call at gate). README rewritten plain-language for non-engineers (Key terms glossary); spec-format bar extended to require readable vocabulary (separate commit); issue #182 filed for universal-governance auto-injection. Human-ratified — HITL spec gate per operations M1 (leash auto-none). File frozen.

Cold sdd-impl-judge IMPLEMENTATION_PASS: all 36 frozen scenarios verified by independently re-derived oracle + node:test run (48/48, none hollow). Pure/IO split real (fs only in the readEvents/appendEvent/appendEdgeChecked seam); Tarjan SCC over RAW edges; greedy lowest-id WAW admission so the frontier never holds a WAW pair; correct capstone closure/floor/progress. 5 producer judgment calls all judged faithful. Delivered plugins/sdd/skills/mission-graph/ (zero-dep .mts engine + tests + SKILL/README) + one-line verify:specs wiring; pnpm verify green. Human-ratified (HITL impl gate, leash auto-none). FINDING (deferred to Op1.M2): the frontier 'node' field is a low-information constant (the vertex-kind, always Mission) because 'node' is OVERLOADED — SDD spec-node (the SSA atom a mission owns) vs mission-graph vertex (Mission/Operation); the design brief's ready-schema used the former reading, the frozen spec glossary pinned the latter. README enumerated 7 frontier fields vs the .feature's 8 (aligned this commit). Disambiguate the term + decide whether the frontier should carry the owned spec-node at the M2 distillation (glossary/TERMINOLOGY/ADRs); filed as a follow-up issue. Doctrine-loop process note: the spec-gate alignment check graded ALIGNED but missed the 7-vs-8 field-list mismatch — candidate for a mechanical spec.md-vs-.feature field-enumeration check.
